candidates.label: Live Tweeting with Location
candidates.description: Posts frequently self‑identify as live tweets from a named location while describing ongoing activities.

citations.text: I'm "live" tweeting tonight from @HGBrewery I'm drinking a beer.
Live tweeting @HGBrewery I'm halfway done with my steak and beer 2.
Live tweeting @HGBrewery Sun is blazing through window and blinding me. Bartender loaned m prescription Ray Bans. http://t.co/ftVDi19quq
I'll be live tweeting the Government shutdown. So far the only thing I've noticed is the battery in my noise canceling headphones is dead.

candidates.scope_note: Occurs across multiple non‑consecutive posts within the batch.
candidates.boundary_note: Not every post that mentions activity or location uses the live‑tweet phrasing; some are plain updates without 'live'.

candidates.label: "Martini Time" Phrase Repetition
candidates.description: The phrase "martini time" appears verbatim in separate posts months apart, signaling a recurring personal cue.

citations.text: I got two olives and a couple of limes, Guessin' that means it's martini time.

candidates.scope_note: Found in two separate posts dated April and September, indicating a pattern.
candidates.boundary_note: The phrase is not used outside these instances and may be idiosyncratic to this author.

candidates.label: "Pay It Forward People" Recurrence
candidates.description: A set phrase "Pay it forward people" is repeated in distinct contexts to emphasize altruistic actions.

citations.text: Sometimes people go to a public restroom and leave a drink for the next visitor. Paying it forward people.
The only coffee in the hotel room this morning was decaffeinated. I made it just so I could throw it away. Pay it forward people.

candidates.scope_note: Appears in at least two posts, suggesting a recurring expression.
candidates.boundary_note: It appears only in contexts involving sharing a drink, not in all posts about generosity.
